figures.caption: Table 1. Comparison with state-of-the-art methods on the ShapeNet In-Context dataset [10]. For reconstruction, denoising, and registration, we report Chamfer Distance [9] loss (×1000). For part segmentation (Part.), we report mIoU. Since PIC [10] does not provide the details of specific multi-task heads, we tested with our own designed multi-task heads, which are marked with †. Bold: the best results. Underline: the second-best results.
Figure 7. Visualization results on denoising task compared with the PIC [10] and our approach.

Table 2. Comparison of generalization ability with state-of-theart methods on the ModelNet40 In-Context (M.) and ScanObjectNN In-Context (S.) datasets. We report task-averaged Chamfer Distance [9] (×1000).

Figure 3. Qualitative comparison on ShapeNet In-Context dataset. From left to right: input point clouds, PIC-Cat [10] results, our DeformPIC results, and ground truth. Our method achieves better geometric detail preservation, improved structural coherence, and fewer visual artifacts across diverse tasks.
Figure 6. Visualization results on reconstruction task compared with the PIC [10] and our approach.

Figure 2. Overall framework of DeformPIC. Our model consists of two core components. The Deformation Extraction Network (DEN) extracts the geometric transformation from an example pair (prompt input→prompt target) into a task embedding. This embedding then modulates the Deformation Transfer Network (DTN), thereby guiding it to apply the same transformation to a new query input to produce the final prediction. The entire network is trained by minimizing the Chamfer Distance [9] against the ground truth.
Figure 5. Visualization results on registration task compared with the PIC [10] and our approach.

Figure 4. Task Feature Visualization of DeformPIC on the ShapeNet / ModelNet40 / ScanObjectNN In-Context datasets. We use t-SNE [29] to reduce the dimensionality of the task features to a 2D space and visualize the distributions of task features.
Figure 8. Visualization results on part segmentation task compared with the PIC [10] and our approach.
